title: The Park Bench Parliament
text_zh: 每天早上七点半到九点之间，小区旁边的公园里有一个非正式的「长椅议会」在召开。参与者是六到八位退休老人，他们固定坐在同一排三把长椅上，讨论的议题涵盖了从国际形势到社区物业管理的各个层面。我在跑步路过时断断续续地旁听了几个月，发现这个议会有着严格的非成文规则。首先，座位有默认的分配方式：最左边的长椅属于「时事评论组」，主要由两位退休教师主持；中间的长椅是「民生组」，讨论菜价、天气和医院排队等实际话题；最右边的长椅则是「文艺组」，成员以下棋和讨论京剧为主。三个小组之间偶尔会出现「联合辩论」——通常发生在某个重大新闻事件以后，三把长椅上的人会暂时合并成一个大圈子。发言权的分配也有微妙的等级制度：资历最老的赵大爷拥有不可挑战的最终发言权，但他很少使用这个特权，大多数时候只是闭着眼睛听别人说话，偶尔用一句简短的评论来为整个讨论定调。九点整，议会准时散会——因为那是公园里开始播放广播体操音乐的时间。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang qī diǎn bàn dào jiǔ diǎn zhī jiān, xiǎoqū pángbiān de gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yí gè fēi zhèngshì de 「chángyǐ yìhuì」 zài zhàokāi. Cānyù zhě shì liù dào bā wèi tuìxiū lǎorén, tāmen gùdìng zuò zài tóng yì pái sān bǎ chángyǐ shàng, tǎolùn de yìtí hángài le cóng guójì xíngshì dào shèqū wùyè guǎnlǐ de gè gè céngmiàn. Wǒ zài pǎobù lù guò shí duànduànxùxù de pángtīng le jǐ gè yuè, fāxiàn zhège yìhuì yǒu zhe yángé de fēi chéngwén guīzé. Shǒuxiān, zuòwèi yǒu mòrèn de fēnpèi fāngshì: zuì zuǒbiān de chángyǐ shǔyú 「shíshì pínglùn zǔ」, zhǔyào yóu liǎng wèi tuìxiū jiàoshī zhǔchí; zhōngjiān de chángyǐ shì 「mínshēng zǔ」, tǎolùn cài jià, tiānqì hé yīyuàn páiduì děng shíjì huàtí; zuì yòubiān de chángyǐ zé shì 「wényì zǔ」, chéngyuán yǐ xiàqí hé tǎolùn jīngjù wéi zhǔ. Sān gè xiǎozǔ zhī jiān ǒu'ěr huì chūxiàn 「liánhé biànlùn」 — tōngcháng fāshēng zài mǒu gè zhòngdà xīnwén shìjiàn yǐhòu, sān bǎ chángyǐ shàng de rén huì zànshí hébìng chéng yí gè dà quānzi. Fāyánquán de fēnpèi yě yǒu wēimiào de děngjí zhìdù: zīlì zuì lǎo de Zhào dàye yōngyǒu bùkě tiǎozhàn de zuìzhōng fāyánquán, dàn tā hěn shǎo shǐyòng zhège tèquán, dà duōshù shíhou zhǐ shì bì zhe yǎnjīng tīng biérén shuōhuà, ǒu'ěr yòng yí jù jiǎnduǎn de pínglùn lái wèi zhěng gè tǎolùn dìng diào. Jiǔ diǎn zhěng, yìhuì zhǔnshí sànhuì — yīnwèi nà shì gōngyuán lǐ kāishǐ bōfàng guǎngbō tǐcāo yīnyuè de shíjiān.
text_en: Every morning between 7:30 and 9:00, an informal 'bench parliament' convenes in the park next to our residential compound. The participants are six to eight retirees who always sit on the same row of three benches, discussing topics ranging from international affairs to community property management. I've been intermittently eavesdropping while jogging past for several months and discovered this parliament operates by strict unwritten rules. First, seating has a default allocation: the leftmost bench belongs to the 'Current Affairs Commentary Group,' mainly moderated by two retired teachers; the middle bench is the 'Livelihood Group,' discussing vegetable prices, weather, and hospital queues; the rightmost bench is the 'Arts Group,' whose members mainly play chess and discuss Peking opera. Occasionally a 'joint debate' occurs between the three groups — usually following major news events, when the three benches temporarily merge into one large circle. The distribution of speaking rights also follows a subtle hierarchy: Elder Zhao, with the longest seniority, holds unchallenged final speaking authority, but he rarely exercises this privilege, mostly sitting with eyes closed listening to others, occasionally setting the tone for the entire discussion with a brief comment. At nine o'clock sharp, the parliament adjourns punctually — because that's when the park's broadcast calisthenics music begins playing.

questions.q_zh: 中间长椅的小组讨论什么话题？
questions.q_en: What topics does the middle bench group discuss?

options.text: 菜价、天气和医院排队等民生话题
options.pinyin: cài jià, tiānqì hé yīyuàn páiduì děng mínshēng huàtí
options.text_en: Livelihood topics like prices, weather, hospital queues

questions.q_zh: 议会为什么在九点准时散会？
questions.q_en: Why does the parliament adjourn at exactly nine o'clock?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi lǎorénmen yào qù mǎi cài
options.text_en: Because the elders need to buy groceries

options.text: 因为公园开始播放广播体操音乐
options.pinyin: yīnwèi gōngyuán kāishǐ bōfàng guǎngbō tǐcāo yīnyuè
options.text_en: Because the park starts playing calisthenics music

title: The Cartography of Puddles
text_zh: 每次下雨以后，人行道上的水坑都会出现在完全相同的位置。这些水坑是城市地表微地形的忠实记录者，标注出了每一处肉眼难以察觉的凹陷。我经常路过的那条街上有七个固定的水坑点位，它们的形状和大小与降雨量成正比例变化：小雨只能填满其中三个最深的凹陷，中雨能激活全部七个，暴雨则会让相邻的几个水坑连成一片。我最喜欢的一个水坑在街角的一棵梧桐树下，它的形状近似心形，是树根常年隆起导致周围路面微微下沉形成的。每次雨后，这个心形水坑会倒映出梧桐树的树冠轮廓，像一面嵌在地面上的镜子。清洁工每天早上六点半开始扫街，他的扫帚会暂时打破水坑的平静，但一个小时以后水面又恢复了镜面般的状态。到了下午，太阳开始蒸发水坑里的水，水坑从边缘开始收缩，留下一圈圈越来越小的干燥痕迹——像是微缩版的退潮。我有时候会想，如果外星人只能通过水坑来了解地球的城市，他们会读到一部怎样的故事。
text_pinyin: Měi cì xià yǔ yǐhòu, rénxíngdào shàng de shuǐkēng dōu huì chūxiàn zài wánquán xiāngtóng de wèizhì. Zhèxiē shuǐkēng shì chéngshì dìbiǎo wēi dìxíng de zhōngshí jìlù zhě, biāozhù chū le měi yí chù ròuyǎn nányǐ chájué de āoxiàn. Wǒ jīngcháng lùguò de nà tiáo jiē shàng yǒu qī gè gùdìng de shuǐkēng diǎnwèi, tāmen de xíngzhuàng hé dàxiǎo yǔ jiàngyǔliàng chéng zhèng bǐlì biànhuà: xiǎo yǔ zhǐ néng tián mǎn qízhōng sān gè zuì shēn de āoxiàn, zhōng yǔ néng jīhuó quánbù qī gè, bàoyǔ zé huì ràng xiānglín de jǐ gè shuǐkēng lián chéng yí piàn. Wǒ zuì xǐhuan de yí gè shuǐkēng zài jiē jiǎo de yì kē wútóng shù xià, tā de xíngzhuàng jìnsì xīnxíng, shì shùgēn chángnián lóngqǐ dǎozhì zhōuwéi lùmiàn wēiwēi xiàchén xíngchéng de. Měi cì yǔ hòu, zhège xīnxíng shuǐkēng huì dàoyìng chū wútóng shù de shùguān lúnkuò, xiàng yí miàn qiàn zài dìmiàn shàng de jìngzi. Qīngjié gōng měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn bàn kāishǐ sǎo jiē, tā de sàozhou huì zànshí dǎpò shuǐkēng de píngjìng, dàn yí gè xiǎoshí yǐhòu shuǐmiàn yòu huīfù le jìngmiàn bān de zhuàngtài. Dào le xiàwǔ, tàiyáng kāishǐ zhēngfā shuǐkēng lǐ de shuǐ, shuǐkēng cóng biānyuán kāishǐ shōusuō, liú xià yì quānquān yuè lái yuè xiǎo de gānzào hénjì — xiàng shì wēisuō bǎn de tuìcháo. Wǒ yǒushíhou huì xiǎng, rúguǒ wàixīngrén zhǐ néng tōngguò shuǐkēng lái liǎojiě dìqiú de chéngshì, tāmen huì dú dào yí bù zěnyàng de gùshi.
text_en: After every rain, the puddles on the sidewalk appear in exactly the same positions. These puddles are faithful recorders of the city's surface micro-topography, marking every depression imperceptible to the naked eye. The street I frequently pass has seven fixed puddle locations, their shape and size changing in direct proportion to rainfall: a light rain fills only the three deepest depressions, moderate rain activates all seven, and a downpour connects several adjacent puddles into a single sheet. My favorite puddle is under a plane tree at the street corner, nearly heart-shaped, formed by tree roots that over the years pushed up, causing the surrounding pavement to sink slightly. After each rain, this heart-shaped puddle reflects the canopy outline of the plane tree, like a mirror embedded in the ground. The street cleaner starts sweeping at 6:30 every morning; his broom temporarily disrupts the puddle's calm, but an hour later the surface returns to its mirror state. In the afternoon, the sun begins evaporating the water, and the puddle shrinks from the edges, leaving progressively smaller rings of dry traces — like a miniature receding tide. Sometimes I wonder: if aliens could only understand Earth's cities through puddles, what kind of story would they read.

questions.q_zh: 作者最喜欢的水坑是什么形状的？
questions.q_en: What shape is the author's favorite puddle?

questions.q_zh: 暴雨时街上的水坑会发生什么变化？
questions.q_en: What happens to the street puddles during a heavy rainstorm?

options.pinyin: xiānglín de shuǐkēng lián chéng yí piàn
options.text_en: Adjacent puddles merge into one sheet

options.pinyin: zhǐ yǒu sān gè shuǐkēng yǒu shuǐ

options.text: 水坑变得更深但不变大
options.pinyin: shuǐkēng biàn de gèng shēn dàn bú biàn dà
options.text_en: Puddles get deeper but don't expand

title: The Scent Calendar of a Street
text_zh: 我上班经过的那条街有一个随时间变化的气味日历。早上六点半，最先弥漫开来的是早餐店蒸笼里的蒸汽——面粉和酵母混合的温暖气息，夹杂着豆浆煮沸时特有的焦甜。七点整，街角的干洗店开门了，洗衣液和熨斗蒸汽的化学清香突然加入了空气中的「乐谱」。八点左右是汽车尾气最浓的时段，柴油公交车排放的气味像一层灰色的幕布暂时覆盖了其他所有味道。上午十点，两家餐馆开始准备午餐：一家川菜馆的辣椒炒料味从排风扇中涌出，浓烈而直接；隔壁粤菜馆的炖汤香气则是缓慢渗透的，需要几分钟才能识别出其中的人参和枸杞成分。下午两点是气味最淡的时段，只剩下道路两旁法国梧桐树叶在阳光下蒸腾的微微苦涩。傍晚六点，烤串摊出来了，孜然和烟熏味宣告了这条街的夜间模式正式开启。我发现，如果连续几天某个固定时段的气味消失了，通常意味着那家店在装修或者换了老板——气味是这条街最敏感的「营业晴雨表」。
text_pinyin: Wǒ shàngbān jīngguò de nà tiáo jiē yǒu yí gè suí shíjiān biànhuà de qìwèi rìlì. Zǎoshang liù diǎn bàn, zuìxiān mímàn kāilái de shì zǎocān diàn zhēnglóng lǐ de zhēngqì — miànfěn hé jiàomǔ hùnhé de wēnnuǎn qìxī, jiázá zhe dòujiāng zhǔ fèi shí tèyǒu de jiāo tián. Qī diǎn zhěng, jiē jiǎo de gānxǐ diàn kāi mén le, xǐyī yè hé yùndǒu zhēngqì de huàxué qīngxiāng tūrán jiārù le kōngqì zhōng de 「yuèpǔ」. Bā diǎn zuǒyòu shì qìchē wěiqì zuì nóng de shíduàn, cháiyóu gōngjiāo chē páifàng de qìwèi xiàng yì céng huīsè de mùbù zànshí fùgài le qítā suǒyǒu wèidào. Shàngwǔ shí diǎn, liǎng jiā cānguǎn kāishǐ zhǔnbèi wǔcān: yì jiā Chuāncài guǎn de làjiāo chǎo liào wèi cóng páifēngshàn zhōng yǒng chū, nóngliè ér zhíjiē; gébì Yuècài guǎn de dùn tāng xiāngqì zé shì huǎnmàn shèntòu de, xūyào jǐ fēnzhōng cái néng shíbié chū qízhōng de rénshēn hé gǒuqǐ chéngfèn. Xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn shì qìwèi zuì dàn de shíduàn, zhǐ shèng xià dàolù liǎng páng Fǎguó wútóng shù yè zài yángguāng xià zhēngténg de wēiwēi kǔsè. Bàngwǎn liù diǎn, kǎo chuàn tān chūlái le, zīrán hé yānxūn wèi xuāngào le zhè tiáo jiē de yèjiān móshì zhèngshì kāiqǐ. Wǒ fāxiàn, rúguǒ liánxù jǐ tiān mǒu gè gùdìng shíduàn de qìwèi xiāoshī le, tōngcháng yìwèi zhe nà jiā diàn zài zhuāngxiū huòzhě huàn le lǎobǎn — qìwèi shì zhè tiáo jiē zuì mǐngǎn de 「yíngyè qíngyǔbiǎo」.
text_en: The street I pass on my way to work has a scent calendar that changes with time. At 6:30 AM, the first smell to spread is the steam from the breakfast shop's bamboo steamers — the warm breath of flour and yeast mixed together, tinged with the distinctive caramelized sweetness of boiling soy milk. At seven sharp, the corner dry cleaner opens, and the chemical freshness of detergent and iron steam suddenly joins the air's 'score.' Around eight is the peak of car exhaust, with diesel bus emissions hanging like a gray curtain that temporarily masks all other scents. At ten AM, two restaurants begin preparing lunch: a Sichuan restaurant's stir-fried chili spice pours from the exhaust fan, intense and direct; the neighboring Cantonese restaurant's stewed soup aroma permeates slowly, requiring a few minutes before you can identify the ginseng and goji berry components. Two in the afternoon is the faintest scent period, with only the slight bitterness of plane tree leaves transpiring in the sunlight along both sides of the road. At six in the evening, the kebab stalls appear, and cumin and smoke announce that this street's night mode has officially begun. I've found that if a scent from a fixed time slot disappears for several consecutive days, it usually means that shop is being renovated or has changed owners — scent is this street's most sensitive 'business barometer.'

questions.q_zh: 下午两点的街道气味有什么特点？
questions.q_en: What characterizes the street's scent at 2 PM?

options.pinyin: làjiāo hé zīrán wèi zuì nóng
shì qìwèi zuì dàn de shíduàn
zǎocān diàn de zhēngqì zuì nóng

questions.q_zh: 气味消失几天通常意味着什么？
questions.q_en: What does the disappearance of a scent for several days usually mean?

options.text: 那家店在装修或者换了老板
options.pinyin: nà jiā diàn zài zhuāngxiū huòzhě huàn le lǎobǎn
options.text_en: That shop is renovating or changed owners

options.pinyin: nǐ de bízi chū le wèntí

title: The Biography of a Manhole Cover
text_zh: 城市的人行道上散布着各种各样的井盖，大多数人从不会低头看它们一眼。但如果你仔细观察，每一个井盖都在讲述一段城市基础设施的历史。我在上班路上数过，短短两百米的人行道上竟然分布着十四个不同类型的井盖。最古老的一个是铸铁材质的，上面铸着「某某市排水公司一九八五」的字样，花纹是传统的菱形格子，边缘已经被磨得圆润光滑。最新的一个是不锈钢的，表面有防滑纹路和一个二维码标识——扫一扫就能查到这个井盖下面管道的类型和维护记录。不同功能的井盖有不同的标志：水字标志是供水管道，火字标志是消防栓接口，带闪电符号的是电力管线，写着「雨」字的是雨水排放系统。这些井盖共同构成了一幅地下管网的地面投影图——城市脚下隐藏着另一个平行的世界，由水管、电缆、光纤和排水管道编织而成。每次路过那个一九八五年的老井盖，我都会想：这块铁圆盘已经在这里守候了四十多年，承受了数不清的脚步和车轮碾压，却从来没有离开过自己的岗位。
text_pinyin: Chéngshì de rénxíngdào shàng sànbù zhe gè zhǒng gè yàng de jǐnggài, dà duōshù rén cóng bú huì dītóu kàn tāmen yì yǎn. Dàn rúguǒ nǐ zǐxì guānchá, měi yí gè jǐnggài dōu zài jiǎngshù yí duàn chéngshì jīchǔ shèshī de lìshǐ. Wǒ zài shàngbān lù shàng shǔ guò, duǎnduǎn liǎng bǎi mǐ de rénxíngdào shàng jìngrán fēnbù zhe shísì gè bùtóng lèixíng de jǐnggài. Zuì gǔlǎo de yí gè shì zhùtiě cáizhì de, shàngmiàn zhù zhe 「mǒumǒu shì páishuǐ gōngsī yī jiǔ bā wǔ」 de zìyàng, huāwén shì chuántǒng de língxíng gézi, biānyuán yǐjīng bèi mó de yuánrùn guānghuá. Zuì xīn de yí gè shì bùxiùgāng de, biǎomiàn yǒu fáng huá wénlù hé yí gè èrwéimǎ biāoshí — sǎo yì sǎo jiù néng chá dào zhège jǐnggài xiàmiàn guǎndào de lèixíng hé wéihù jìlù. Bùtóng gōngnéng de jǐnggài yǒu bùtóng de biāozhì: shuǐ zì biāozhì shì gōngshuǐ guǎndào, huǒ zì biāozhì shì xiāofángshuān jiēkǒu, dài shǎndiàn fúhào de shì diànlì guǎnxiàn, xiě zhe 「yǔ」 zì de shì yǔshuǐ páifàng xìtǒng. Zhèxiē jǐnggài gòngtóng gòuchéng le yì fú dìxià guǎnwǎng de dìmiàn tóuyǐng tú — chéngshì jiǎo xià yǐncáng zhe lìng yí gè píngxíng de shìjiè, yóu shuǐguǎn, diànlǎn, guāngxiān hé páishuǐ guǎndào biānzhī ér chéng. Měi cì lùguò nàge yī jiǔ bā wǔ nián de lǎo jǐnggài, wǒ dōu huì xiǎng: zhè kuài tiě yuánpán yǐjīng zài zhèlǐ shǒuhòu le sìshí duō nián, chéngshòu le shǔ bù qīng de jiǎobù hé chēlún niǎnyā, què cónglái méiyǒu líkāi guò zìjǐ de gǎngwèi.
text_en: City sidewalks are scattered with all kinds of manhole covers, which most people never bother to glance down at. But if you observe carefully, each manhole cover tells a story of urban infrastructure history. I've counted on my commute: a mere two hundred meters of sidewalk has fourteen different types of manhole covers. The oldest is cast iron, with the inscription 'XX City Drainage Company 1985,' featuring a traditional diamond lattice pattern with edges worn smooth and rounded. The newest is stainless steel, with anti-slip texture and a QR code — scan it and you can look up the type and maintenance records of the pipe beneath this cover. Different function covers bear different markings: 'water' indicates the water supply, 'fire' marks a fire hydrant connection, a lightning symbol means electrical conduits, and the character for 'rain' marks the stormwater drainage system. Together these covers form a surface projection map of the underground pipe network — beneath the city's feet hides another parallel world, woven from water pipes, cables, fiber optics, and drainage conduits. Every time I pass that 1985 manhole cover, I think: this iron disc has been standing guard here for over forty years, bearing countless footsteps and tire rolls, yet has never left its post.

questions.q_zh: 最新的井盖上有什么特别的标识？
questions.q_en: What special marking does the newest manhole cover have?

questions.q_zh: 两百米的人行道上有多少个不同类型的井盖？
questions.q_en: How many different types of manhole covers are on the 200-meter sidewalk?

title: The Rooftop Garden at Dusk
text_zh: 我们那栋老公寓楼的天台上有一个自发形成的花园，没有人知道是谁先开始在那里种花的。十几个花盆散落在天台的各个角落，里面种着各种各样的植物：三棵矮小的盆栽柑橘树、一排迷迭香、几丛不知名的多肉植物，还有一株攀爬到水箱外壁上的牵牛花。黄昏是这个天台花园最美的时刻——夕阳把所有的植物都染上了一层金色，花盆的阴影在水泥地面上拉出长长的几何线条。我发现住在六楼的陈阿姨每天傍晚都会上来浇花，但她只浇自己种的那几盆月季，对其他人的植物视而不见。五楼的小李则在每个周末早上悄悄地给所有植物施肥——包括不属于他的。这种无言的合作和微妙的领地意识构成了天台花园独特的社会生态。有一次台风过后，所有花盆都被大风吹倒了，第二天早上整栋楼的住户不约而同地上来帮忙扶正花盆、清理碎片。那是我第一次看到天台花园的所有「股东」同时出现——原来这个小小的空中花园牵动着至少八户人家的心。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen nà dòng lǎo gōngyù lóu de tiāntái shàng yǒu yí gè zìfā xíngchéng de huāyuán, méiyǒu rén zhīdào shì shéi xiān kāishǐ zài nàlǐ zhòng huā de. Shí jǐ gè huāpén sànluò zài tiāntái de gè gè jiǎoluò, lǐmiàn zhòng zhe gè zhǒng gè yàng de zhíwù: sān kē ǎixiǎo de pénzāi gānjú shù, yì pái mídié xiāng, jǐ cóng bù zhī míng de duōròu zhíwù, hái yǒu yì zhū pānpá dào shuǐxiāng wài bì shàng de qiānniúhuā. Huánghūn shì zhège tiāntái huāyuán zuì měi de shíkè — xīyáng bǎ suǒyǒu de zhíwù dōu rǎn shàng le yì céng jīnsè, huāpén de yīnyǐng zài shuǐní dìmiàn shàng lā chū cháng cháng de jǐhé xiàntiáo. Wǒ fāxiàn zhù zài liù lóu de Chén āyí měi tiān bàngwǎn dōu huì shànglái jiāo huā, dàn tā zhǐ jiāo zìjǐ zhòng de nà jǐ pén yuèjì, duì qítā rén de zhíwù shì'érbújiàn. Wǔ lóu de Xiǎo Lǐ zé zài měi gè zhōumò zǎoshang qiāoqiāo de gěi suǒyǒu zhíwù shī féi — bāokuò bù shǔyú tā de. Zhè zhǒng wúyán de hézuò hé wēimiào de lǐngdì yìshí gòuchéng le tiāntái huāyuán dútè de shèhuì shēngtài. Yǒu yí cì táifēng guòhòu, suǒyǒu huāpén dōu bèi dà fēng chuī dào le, dì èr tiān zǎoshang zhěng dòng lóu de zhùhù bùyuēértóng de shànglái bāngmáng fú zhèng huāpén, qīnglǐ suìpiàn. Nà shì wǒ dì yī cì kàn dào tiāntái huāyuán de suǒyǒu 「gǔdōng」 tóngshí chūxiàn — yuánlái zhège xiǎoxiǎo de kōngzhōng huāyuán qiāndòng zhe zhìshǎo bā hù rénjiā de xīn.
text_en: On our old apartment building's rooftop there's a spontaneously formed garden — nobody knows who first started planting flowers there. Over a dozen flowerpots are scattered across the rooftop's corners, growing all kinds of plants: three small potted citrus trees, a row of rosemary, a few clumps of unidentified succulents, and a morning glory that has climbed up the outside of the water tank. Dusk is this rooftop garden's most beautiful moment — the setting sun coats every plant in gold, and the pots' shadows stretch long geometric lines across the concrete. I've noticed Auntie Chen from the sixth floor comes up every evening to water flowers, but she only waters her own rose bushes, ignoring everyone else's plants. Xiao Li from the fifth floor quietly fertilizes all the plants every weekend morning — including ones that aren't his. This wordless cooperation and subtle territorial awareness compose the rooftop garden's unique social ecology. After a typhoon once knocked over all the pots, the next morning residents from the entire building came up simultaneously to help right the pots and clear debris — without prior arrangement. That was the first time I saw all the rooftop garden's 'shareholders' appear at once — it turned out this small aerial garden had at least eight households' hearts invested in it.

questions.q_zh: 五楼的小李怎么照顾天台的植物？
questions.q_en: How does Xiao Li from the fifth floor care for the rooftop plants?

options.text: 每个周末给所有植物施肥
options.pinyin: měi gè zhōumò gěi suǒyǒu zhíwù shī féi

questions.q_zh: 台风以后发生了什么事？

options.text_en: Nobody came to tend the garden

options.text: 整栋楼的住户都来帮忙
options.pinyin: zhěng dòng lóu de zhùhù dōu lái bāngmáng
options.text_en: Residents from the whole building came to help

options.pinyin: zhǐ yǒu Chén āyí lái le

title: The Commuter's Meditation
text_zh: 每天早上七点十五分，我站在公交车站等二十七路公交车。等待的十二分钟里，我发展出了一种属于通勤者的冥想方式。首先是观察同一站等车的常客们的细微变化：穿灰色大衣的中年女士今天换了一双新鞋，说明昨天可能去逛了街；戴蓝色耳机的男生最近两周改喝了美式咖啡，之前一直是拿铁。公交车到来的前两分钟是最有意思的——经验丰富的常客能凭借远处的引擎声判断来的是老旧的柴油车还是新型的电动公交，从而提前调整自己的站位以便在车门打开时占据最佳上车位置。车上的座位选择也有讲究：靠窗的位置最先被占满，然后是靠过道的，最后才是中间的连体座。我总是选择右后方靠窗的位置，因为那里的视角能看到沿途最多的街景。二十七分钟的车程里，窗外的景物从老城区的低矮房屋逐渐过渡到商务区的玻璃幕墙，像是在翻阅一本城市的横截面画册。到站下车的瞬间，冥想结束，现实的节奏重新接管了大脑。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang qī diǎn shíwǔ fēn, wǒ zhàn zài gōngjiāo chē zhàn děng èrshíqī lù gōngjiāo chē. Děngdài de shí'èr fēnzhōng lǐ, wǒ fāzhǎn chū le yì zhǒng shǔyú tōngqín zhě de míngxiǎng fāngshì. Shǒuxiān shì guānchá tóng yí zhàn děng chē de chángkè men de xìwēi biànhuà: chuān huīsè dàyī de zhōngnián nǚshì jīntiān huàn le yì shuāng xīn xié, shuōmíng zuótiān kěnéng qù guàng le jiē; dài lánsè ěrjī de nánshēng zuìjìn liǎng zhōu gǎi hē le Měishì kāfēi, zhīqián yìzhí shì nàtiě. Gōngjiāo chē dàolái de qián liǎng fēnzhōng shì zuì yǒu yìsi de — jīngyàn fēngfù de chángkè néng píngjié yuǎnchù de yǐnqíng shēng pànduàn lái de shì lǎojiù de cháiyóu chē háishi xīnxíng de diàndòng gōngjiāo, cóng'ér tíqián tiáozhěng zìjǐ de zhànwèi yǐbiàn zài chē mén dǎkāi shí zhànjù zuìjiā shàng chē wèizhì. Chē shàng de zuòwèi xuǎnzé yě yǒu jiǎngjiù: kào chuāng de wèizhì zuì xiān bèi zhàn mǎn, ránhòu shì kào guòdào de, zuìhòu cái shì zhōngjiān de liántǐ zuò. Wǒ zǒng shì xuǎnzé yòu hòufāng kào chuāng de wèizhì, yīnwèi nàlǐ de shìjiǎo néng kàn dào yántú zuì duō de jiējǐng. Èrshíqī fēnzhōng de chēchéng lǐ, chuāng wài de jǐngwù cóng lǎo chéngqū de dīǎi fángwū zhújiàn guòdù dào shāngwù qū de bōli mùqiáng, xiàng shì zài fānyuè yì běn chéngshì de héngjiémiàn huàcè. Dào zhàn xià chē de shùnjiān, míngxiǎng jiéshù, xiànshí de jiézòu chóngxīn jiēguǎn le dànǎo.
text_en: Every morning at 7:15 I stand at the bus stop waiting for the Route 27 bus. During the twelve-minute wait, I've developed a form of commuter's meditation. First I observe the subtle changes in fellow regular passengers at the same stop: the middle-aged woman in the gray coat is wearing new shoes today, suggesting she may have gone shopping yesterday; the young man with blue headphones switched to Americano coffee in the past two weeks — it was always lattes before. The two minutes before the bus arrives are most interesting — experienced regulars can judge from the distant engine sound whether an old diesel bus or new electric bus is coming, and preemptively adjust their position to secure the best boarding spot when the doors open. Seat selection on the bus follows its own logic: window seats fill first, then aisle seats, and lastly the connected middle seats. I always choose the right rear window seat, because that angle provides the most street views along the route. During the twenty-seven-minute ride, the scenery outside transitions gradually from the old district's low-rise buildings to the business district's glass curtain walls, like flipping through a cross-section album of the city. The instant I step off at my stop, the meditation ends, and reality's rhythm resumes command of the brain.

questions.q_zh: 经验丰富的乘客怎么提前判断公交车的类型？
questions.q_en: How do experienced passengers judge the bus type in advance?

options.text: 看手机上的实时公交信息
options.pinyin: kàn shǒujī shàng de shíshí gōngjiāo xìnxī
options.text_en: Check real-time bus info on phone

options.pinyin: píng yuǎnchù de yǐnqíng shēng pànduàn

questions.q_zh: 公交车上哪种座位最后才被坐满？
questions.q_en: Which type of bus seat fills last?

title: The Scaffolding City
text_zh: 有一段时间，我住的那个街区同时有三栋楼在进行外墙翻新，整条街被脚手架和绿色防护网包围了。走在街上就像走在一个临时搭建的隧道里，头顶是密密麻麻的钢管和木板，两侧是半透明的绿色网幕，阳光透过网幕洒进来，给整条街道染上了一层翡翠绿的色调。我很快就习惯了这种独特的光线环境，甚至开始喜欢它——尤其是下午三四点的时候，斜射的阳光穿过防护网上不同大小的网眼，在地面上投射出一种类似水底波纹的光影图案。脚手架改变了街道的声学特性：工人在高处敲打的声音被钢管结构共振放大，形成了一种金属质感的回声；而路人的说话声则被防护网吸收了一部分，显得比平时柔和。三个月以后，脚手架逐渐被拆除，翻新后的外墙露出了崭新的白色涂料。但我却有一种奇怪的失落感——那个绿色的临时城市消失了，一个只存在了九十天的平行世界被永久地拆解，不留下任何痕迹。
text_pinyin: Yǒu yí duàn shíjiān, wǒ zhù de nàge jiēqū tóngshí yǒu sān dòng lóu zài jìnxíng wàiqiáng fānxīn, zhěng tiáo jiē bèi jiǎoshǒujià hé lǜsè fánghù wǎng bāowéi le. Zǒu zài jiē shàng jiù xiàng zǒu zài yí gè línshí dājiàn de suìdào lǐ, tóudǐng shì mìmimámá de gāngguǎn hé mùbǎn, liǎng cè shì bàn tòumíng de lǜsè wǎng mù, yángguāng tòu guò wǎng mù sǎ jìnlái, gěi zhěng tiáo jiēdào rǎn shàng le yì céng fěicuì lǜ de sèdiào. Wǒ hěn kuài jiù xíguàn le zhè zhǒng dútè de guāngxiàn huánjìng, shènzhì kāishǐ xǐhuan tā — yóuqí shì xiàwǔ sān sì diǎn de shíhou, xiéshè de yángguāng chuān guò fánghù wǎng shàng bùtóng dàxiǎo de wǎngyǎn, zài dìmiàn shàng tóushè chū yì zhǒng lèisì shuǐdǐ bōwén de guāngyǐng tú'àn. Jiǎoshǒujià gǎibiàn le jiēdào de shēngxué tèxìng: gōngrén zài gāochù qiāodǎ de shēngyīn bèi gāngguǎn jiégòu gòngzhèn fàngdà, xíngchéng le yì zhǒng jīnshǔ zhìgǎn de huíshēng; ér lùrén de shuōhuà shēng zé bèi fánghù wǎng xīshōu le yí bùfèn, xiǎn de bǐ píngshí róuhé. Sān gè yuè yǐhòu, jiǎoshǒujià zhújiàn bèi chāichú, fānxīn hòu de wàiqiáng lùchū le zhǎnxīn de báisè túliào. Dàn wǒ què yǒu yì zhǒng qíguài de shīluò gǎn — nàge lǜsè de línshí chéngshì xiāoshī le, yí gè zhǐ cúnzài le jiǔshí tiān de píngxíng shìjiè bèi yǒngjiǔ de chāijiě, bù liú xià rènhé hénjì.
text_en: For a while, three buildings in my neighborhood were simultaneously undergoing exterior renovation, and the entire street was surrounded by scaffolding and green safety nets. Walking down the street was like walking through a temporarily constructed tunnel, with dense steel pipes and planks overhead, semi-transparent green netting on both sides, and sunlight filtering through the nets to cast a jade-green tint over the entire street. I quickly grew accustomed to this unique light environment, and even began to enjoy it — especially around three or four in the afternoon, when angled sunlight passed through different-sized mesh openings in the safety net, projecting a light-and-shadow pattern on the ground resembling underwater ripples. The scaffolding altered the street's acoustic properties: the sound of workers hammering at height was amplified by the steel pipe structure's resonance, creating a metallic-textured echo; while pedestrians' voices were partially absorbed by the safety net, sounding softer than usual. Three months later the scaffolding was gradually dismantled, revealing the renovated walls' brand-new white paint. But I felt a strange sense of loss — that green temporary city had vanished, a parallel world that existed for only ninety days permanently disassembled, leaving no trace behind.

questions.q_zh: 脚手架怎么改变了街道的声音？
questions.q_en: How did the scaffolding change the street's sounds?

options.pinyin: ràng jiēdào biàn de gèng ānjìng

options.text: 工人的声音被共振放大，路人说话声变柔和
options.pinyin: gōngrén de shēngyīn bèi gòngzhèn fàngdà, lùrén shuōhuà shēng biàn róuhé
options.text_en: Worker sounds amplified by resonance, pedestrian voices softened

options.pinyin: suǒyǒu shēngyīn dōu bèi píngbì le
zhǐ néng tīng dào fēng shēng

questions.q_zh: 脚手架拆除以后作者有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How did the author feel after the scaffolding was removed?

options.pinyin: yì zhǒng qíguài de shīluò gǎn

title: The Library of Sounds
text_zh: 我们社区的图书馆其实并不安静——它只是用一种特殊的方式在低声说话。空调系统全年运转，发出恒定的低频嗡鸣，这个声音如此持久和均匀，以至于大脑会在几分钟内将其过滤为「静音」。但如果你仔细听，会发现空调的声音在不同区域有微妙的差异：二楼的阅览室靠近空调主机，嗡嗡声最明显；一楼的儿童区因为天花板较高，声音显得更空旷和遥远。翻书的声音是图书馆最具标志性的音效——每个人翻页的节奏都不同。对面坐着的一位老先生大约每四十五秒翻一页，频率极其稳定，说明他在阅读一本内容密集的非虚构作品。角落里的年轻女孩翻页速度是他的三倍，大概率在看小说。最安静的时段是工作日的下午两点到四点之间，有时候安静到你能听见墙壁上挂钟秒针走动的声音——「嘀嗒」声在空旷的阅览室里产生了一种微弱的混响，听上去反而比真正的寂静更能让人感到平静。五点钟关门前，管理员轻声说一句「快闭馆了」，这句话在安静的空间里传播的方式格外清晰温柔，像是在对整个图书馆道晚安。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen shèqū de túshūguǎn qíshí bìng bú ānjìng — tā zhǐ shì yòng yì zhǒng tèshū de fāngshì zài dīshēng shuōhuà. Kōngtiáo xìtǒng quán nián yùnzhuǎn, fāchū héngdìng de dīpín wēngmíng, zhège shēngyīn rúcǐ chíjiǔ hé jūnyún, yǐzhìyú dànǎo huì zài jǐ fēnzhōng nèi jiāng qí guòlǜ wéi 「jìngyīn」. Dàn rúguǒ nǐ zǐxì tīng, huì fāxiàn kōngtiáo de shēngyīn zài bùtóng qūyù yǒu wēimiào de chāyì: èr lóu de yuèlǎnshì kàojìn kōngtiáo zhǔjī, wēngwēng shēng zuì míngxiǎn; yī lóu de értóng qū yīnwèi tiānhuābǎn jiào gāo, shēngyīn xiǎn de gèng kōngkuàng hé yáoyuǎn. Fān shū de shēngyīn shì túshūguǎn zuì jù biāozhìxìng de yīnxiào — měi gè rén fān yè de jiézòu dōu bùtóng. Duìmiàn zuò zhe de yí wèi lǎo xiānsheng dàyuē měi sìshíwǔ miǎo fān yí yè, pínlǜ jíqí wěndìng, shuōmíng tā zài yuèdú yì běn nèiróng mìjí de fēi xūgòu zuòpǐn. Jiǎoluò lǐ de niánqīng nǚhái fān yè sùdù shì tā de sān bèi, dà gàilǜ zài kàn xiǎoshuō. Zuì ānjìng de shíduàn shì gōngzuò rì de xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn dào sì diǎn zhī jiān, yǒushíhou ānjìng dào nǐ néng tīngjiàn qiángbì shàng guàzhōng miǎozhēn zǒudòng de shēngyīn — 「dīdā」 shēng zài kōngkuàng de yuèlǎnshì lǐ chǎnshēng le yì zhǒng wēiruò de hùnxiǎng, tīng shàngqù fǎn'ér bǐ zhēnzhèng de jìjìng gèng néng ràng rén gǎndào píngjìng. Wǔ diǎn zhōng guān mén qián, guǎnlǐyuán qīngshēng shuō yí jù 「kuài bì guǎn le」, zhè jù huà zài ānjìng de kōngjiān lǐ chuánbō de fāngshì géwài qīngxī wēnróu, xiàng shì zài duì zhěng gè túshūguǎn dào wǎn'ān.
text_en: Our community library isn't actually quiet — it just speaks in a special way at low volume. The air conditioning runs year-round, emitting a constant low-frequency hum so persistent and uniform that the brain filters it to 'mute' within minutes. But if you listen carefully, you'll find the AC sound differs subtly across areas: the second-floor reading room is near the main unit with the most obvious hum; the first-floor children's section, with its higher ceiling, sounds more spacious and distant. The sound of turning pages is the library's most iconic audio — everyone's page-turning rhythm differs. The elderly gentleman sitting across from me turns a page about every forty-five seconds, extremely steady in frequency, indicating he's reading a dense nonfiction work. The young woman in the corner turns pages three times as fast, most likely reading a novel. The quietest period is weekday afternoons between two and four; sometimes it's so silent you can hear the wall clock's second hand — the ticking creates a faint reverberation in the empty reading room that paradoxically feels more calming than true silence. Before closing at five, the librarian says softly 'we're closing soon,' and the way this phrase propagates through the quiet space is exceptionally clear and gentle, as if saying goodnight to the entire library.

questions.q_zh: 作者怎么判断老先生在读什么类型的书？
questions.q_en: How does the author judge what type of book the old gentleman is reading?

options.pinyin: kàn dào le shū de fēngmiàn

options.text: 根据他翻页的频率判断
options.pinyin: gēnjù tā fān yè de pínlǜ pànduàn

questions.q_zh: 图书馆最安静的时段是什么时候？
questions.q_en: When is the quietest time in the library?

options.text_en: When it opens in the morning

options.text: 工作日下午两点到四点
options.pinyin: gōngzuò rì xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn dào sì diǎn
